screens.name: Shared global navigation
screens.purpose: Orient users and provide immediate access to the two remaining website sections.

ux_rules: Keep both remaining destinations visible rather than hiding them behind a hamburger or sheet.
Use a conventional brand link to home, semantic nav and links, and aria-current on the active destination.
Use typography and a two-pixel bottom indicator for active state instead of filled pill backgrounds.
Give every brand and navigation link a minimum 44px touch target and visible focus treatment.
Keep the header in one 64px row at 390px and above with no horizontal overflow.
Remove obsolete destination labels without adding explanatory replacement copy.

screen_contexts.layout_intent: A single 64px edge-aligned product header with a left brand/home anchor and right-aligned Canvas and Songs text links.
interaction_risks: Small link padding could leave mobile targets below 44px.
Brand copy and links could collide or wrap at 390px.
Active state could become ambiguous if it relies on subtle color alone.
Duplicating the old filled pills would fail the requested visual direction.
notes: The existing mobile header is 113px tall and its four pill links are only 36px high.

constraints: Follow vercel:nextjs App Router conventions by deleting route page files rather than adding redirects or replacement pages.
Follow vercel:shadcn direction through semantic Tailwind/shadcn tokens; no new component package is needed for two direct links.
Follow vercel:geist direction with restrained weight and tight tracking; do not add another font or use mono for normal navigation.
Keep the existing client boundary because usePathname is needed only in AppNav.
Render One Five Eight as the brand/home link and Canvas and Songs as the only primary nav labels.
Use a one-row h-16 header with consistent page gutters and no forced minimum width.
Use bg-background, text-foreground, text-muted-foreground, border-border, and ring-ring or equivalent existing semantic tokens.
Use aria-current='page' for the active link and a bottom pseudo-element indicator tied to current route state.
Use min-h-11 or equivalent on every interactive target, visible focus-visible outlines, and motion-reduce-safe transitions.
Do not add a hamburger, Sheet, icons, filled nav pills, shadows, gradients, global CSS variables, or new semantic CSS classes.

critical_judgments: The result reads as a modern product header, not a collection of chips.
One Five Eight, Canvas, and Songs fit on a single row at 390px.
The active route is unmistakable through aria-current, foreground contrast, and the bottom indicator.
All three links measure at least 44px high and show keyboard focus.
Desktop and mobile screenshots show no redundant subtitle, stale route label, or avoidable overflow.
must_not_regress: Canvas and Songs remain directly reachable.
Canvas and Songs route content remains unchanged.
Previously removed Quiz and Chords routes remain absent.
No new reusable CSS rule is required to produce the active indicator.

visual_constraints: Header height is 64px on desktop and mobile.
Use a single subtle bottom border and translucent semantic background; no raised container or shadow.
Brand typography is concise Geist Sans with restrained semibold weight and tight tracking.
Navigation uses plain text targets with a two-pixel bottom active indicator.
Use 16px or 20px mobile horizontal gutter and preserve existing larger desktop page-gutter rhythm.
Avoid pills, badges, rounded navigation backgrounds, decorative icons, and more than one emphasis mechanism.
Desktop and 390px mobile document scrollWidth must equal clientWidth.
